hero.headline: See your real MRR without logging into Stripe
hero.subheadline: A single dashboard for solo SaaS founders. MRR, churn, failed payments, and subscription changes on one page. Connect your read-only Stripe key and see everything in 30 seconds.
hero.cta: Connect Stripe and see your MRR

problem.title: Stripe buries the numbers you actually check daily
problem.body: You open Stripe to answer one question: what's MRR today? Four clicks later, you're still not sure. Does the active subscribers count include trials? Is that $29/mo subscription billed annually showing as $29 or $348? Why does the Stripe revenue chart disagree with the subscriptions page? You close the tab and open your spreadsheet, which is two weeks out of date because you forgot to update it. ChartMogul and Baremetrics solve this, but they start at $100+/mo and are built for finance teams at Series A companies. You're a solo founder doing $8k MRR. You don't need cohort retention curves or LTV forecasting. You need to know: what's MRR, who churned yesterday, whose card just failed, and is next month going to be up or down. That's it. Every morning, same four questions. Stripe's dashboard takes five minutes to answer them. A spreadsheet takes manual work you'll skip. Neither is good enough when you're checking the business every day.

solution.title: One page. Four questions. Answered in 30 seconds.
solution.body: Stripe Revenue Tracker connects to your Stripe account with a read-only restricted API key and shows you exactly what you need on one page: current MRR with 7, 30, and 90-day deltas. Yesterday's churn with customer names and tenure. Every subscription currently in payment failure. A forecast for next month based on active subs and known upcoming changes. No webhooks to configure. No ETL. No data warehouse. We poll the Stripe API every 15 minutes and cache locally. Setup takes under two minutes: paste a restricted key, name your account, done. MRR normalization handles the annual-vs-monthly edge cases correctly, so a $348/year subscription shows as $29/mo and nothing double-counts. No write permissions, ever. We can't refund, cancel, or modify anything in your Stripe account, and the restricted key you generate enforces that at the API level. If you're a solo founder on Stripe doing $1k-$50k MRR, this is the dashboard you've been meaning to build.

features.title: Read-only by design
features.body: You generate a restricted API key with read-only scopes and paste it in. We can't modify your subscriptions, issue refunds, or change customer data. The Stripe API enforces this, not our promise. No webhooks to configure, no OAuth flow, no write permissions requested. If we get breached, the worst-case outcome is someone sees your MRR.

features.title: MRR math that reconciles
features.body: Annual subscriptions get normalized to monthly. Trials aren't counted as active revenue. Discounts, proration, and mid-cycle upgrades are handled the way you'd handle them in a spreadsheet if you had time. The MRR number on your dashboard matches the one you'd calculate by hand. No asterisks, no surprise definitions.

features.title: Failed payments and churn, surfaced
features.body: See every subscription currently in payment failure with the retry schedule and last attempt date. See who churned in the last 24 hours with customer name, plan, tenure, and cancellation reason if Stripe captured one. Stop finding out about a $200/mo churn three weeks after it happened.

faqs.q: How is this different from Baremetrics or ChartMogul?
faqs.a: Same core metrics, smaller scope, lower price. Baremetrics starts around $129/mo and is built for teams with finance functions. This is $20/mo, aimed at solo founders doing $1k-$50k MRR who want the four numbers that matter on one page. No cohort analysis, no LTV models, no team seats. If you need those, buy Baremetrics. If you just want to see MRR without opening Stripe, buy this.

Webhooks require you to configure endpoints in Stripe, handle signature verification, and debug delivery failures. That's a support burden for you and us. Polling the Stripe API every 15 minutes gets you data that's fresh enough for a daily-check dashboard and makes setup a two-minute paste-your-key job. If you need sub-minute latency on churn events, this isn't the tool.

faqs.q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
faqs.a: Your account and cached Stripe data are deleted within 30 days of cancellation. You can also delete everything immediately from the settings page. We never had write access to your Stripe account, so there's nothing to disconnect on Stripe's side beyond revoking the restricted key, which you can do yourself in the Stripe dashboard in two clicks.

cta_final.headline: Stop opening Stripe to check MRR
cta_final.body: Connect a read-only key, see your real numbers on one page, cancel anytime.
